item.title: Terraform Commands
item.description: Commands fmt—rewrites config files to canonical Terraform format/style Also flags syntax errors import—imports existing remote objects into the Terraform state state—advanced state management.

item.title: Terraform Debug Logs
item.description: Enable Debug Logging Set env var TF_LOG to TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN or ERROR Also TF_LOG_CORE (core Terraform debug logs), TF_LOG_PROVIDER (provider logs) TF_LOG_PATH—append logs to a file

item.title: Terraform Provisioners
item.description: Overview Provisioners model actions on the local or remote machines Prepare infrastructure for service Should be used as a last resort For actions not directly represented in Terraform Anti-Patterns Pass Data into VMS Instead: utilize e.

item.title: Terraform Settings
item.description: Terraform Block 1 2 3 terraform { #... } Sub-elements: cloud—configures Terraform Cloud backend—configures the Terraform state backend required_version—constrains the Terraform version Constraints of all child modules must also be satisfied required_providers—specifies providers required by the module Includes address and version constraint

item.title: Terraform
item.description: Exam Topics Infrastructure as Code (IAC) Terraform State Terraform Providers Terraform Settings Terraform Provisioners Terraform Commands Terraform Debug Logs

item.title: Terraform Providers
item.description: Overview Allow Terraform to interact with cloud providers, SaaS providers and other APIs Must declare which providers will be used in the config So Terraform can install Configuration Belong in root module Child modules receive config from root module Can use expressions in config arguments, but values must be known at apply-time (e.

item.title: Terraform State
item.description: Overview Database to map configuration resources to real world remote objects Recommendation: use remote state to allow multiple users to apply updates against the same environment Reasons for State Contains metadata, e.
